reviews.review_description:
As someone who loves puzzle games, Tammuz: Blood and Sand had my brain gears turning from start to finish. The puzzles aren’t obvious, but with some careful thought, they’re all completely solvable. The story is intriguing and kept me engaged the whole way through.

What really caught me off guard was my favorite part of the game, and it’s not even a puzzle. It’s the inclusion of an ancient tabletop board game called Ur, nearly 5,000 years old. After a few rounds, I started to understand how to strategically outsmart Tammuz, and that was such a clever and fun touch.

The controls and mechanics can feel a little fidgety at times, but if you’re patient, it’s easy enough to work through. Be sure to watch through the credits for a surprise. 

If you find yourself struggling on a puzzle, check out my walkthrough video on YouTube @misschiefvr.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing Tammuz: Blood and Sand and for the fantastic review, MissChiefVR! I'm thrilled that the puzzles kept you engaged and that you enjoyed the story. It's especially rewarding to hear how much you loved the inclusion of the Royal Game of Ur - watching players learn the strategy to outsmart Tammuz was exactly what I hoped for. I appreciate your patience and your constructive feedback regarding the fidgety controls, and I will definitely be looking into smoothing those mechanics out for future updates. Thank you for creating a walkthrough video to help other players, and for your wonderful support!

reviews.review_title: Too difficult game to be Pegi 3
reviews.review_description:
The game is rated PEGI 3, but the difficulty does not match this rating at all.

Very early in the game, there is a puzzle that is extremely unclear and frustrating. The mechanics are not explained, and it is not obvious what the player is supposed to do. I was unable to progress without external help.

For a game that is supposed to be suitable for all ages, this is very disappointing. It does not feel intuitive or accessible, especially for younger players.

I hope the developers improve the tutorial or adjust the difficulty, as this creates a poor first experience.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback! We understand how frustrating it can be to get stuck early on in a game.

We definitely want you to be able to enjoy the rest of the game. We have a dedicated section for tutorials and puzzle hints on our Discord server, and our community would be more than happy to help you past this section. We hope to see you there!

To clarify, the PEGI 3 rating refers to the safety of the game's content (meaning there is no violence, bad language, or inappropriate themes), rather than the difficulty of the puzzles. We designed Tammuz entirely without words so it can be enjoyed by players across all ages and languages. Because of this, the game relies heavily on visual and tactile discovery, which we know can sometimes be tricky!

reviews.review_description: I enjoyed this puzzle box game. Very similar to The Room type puzzles.  The puzzles were fairly easy and straightforward but were fun.  One star deducted because I did have some problems with controls that made a few puzzles frustrating. I believe these could be fixed and I’ll give it 4 stars.  Adding hand tracking would be nice. I really enjoyed the boardgame at the end. And would have liked to play it even more.   I finished the game in 2 sessions probably about 4 hours. Because of the length of the game I’d wait for it to go on sale.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you so much for playing and for the thoughtful review! We are thrilled that you had fun and especially loved the UR game at the end. Being compared to The Room is a massive compliment for us!

We would love to know which puzzles and controls gave you trouble so we can have them fixed up. If you're up for it, please join our Discord community here:
https://discord.gg/p7tZ5ymKSW
to share your feedback directly with our dev team. Thanks again for giving Tammuz a try!

reviews.review_description:
Game really stands out thanks to its beautiful and creative puzzles.
On first glance, it is not always 100% clear how the puzzles work, but that contributes to the experience.

Dropped 1 star because the game is a bit on the shorter side.
Finished it in about 4 hours.
There is some replayability, thanks to a few bonus levels and both versions of the game of Ur.
And it ends on 'to be continued', so new content might be added in the future. When that happens, I'll probably increase my rating to 5 stars

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing Tammuz: Blood and Sand and for your thoughtful review!
I'm thrilled that you enjoyed the creative puzzles and found replay value in the bonus levels and the Game of UR, we plan to add more extra levels in the near future! and I greatly appreciate your honest feedback.
Thank you again for your wonderful support!

reviews.review_title: Great puzzle game with a great story
reviews.review_description: I was lucky enough to get this in a giveaway but I seriously think it's worth the price.  It has a couple hiccups like accidentally tapping puzzles when you don't mean to and I had one moment I had to move the tiles around a bit for the game to actually run the marble through the track I made but they're just minor annoyances.  The game had just the right level of difficulty on the puzzles and the story was enjoyable.  The mixed reality seemed to work well, but I personally have a boring game area, so I swapped back to regular VR about halfway through.  I loved seeing the Royal Game of Ur (and with additions) as someone who enjoys board games and owns my own copy of that game physically.  There are also replayable puzzles.  Just a great game.  Highly recommend it to anyone who enjoys puzzles.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words and for taking the time to leave such a detailed review! I'm glad that you found the story engaging and the puzzle difficulty satisfying.

It’s especially awesome to hear from a fellow Royal Game of Ur owner! I’m glad the other modes landed well with you!

Whether you're playing in MR or standard VR, I'm just happy you had a great time in the world of Tammuz. Thanks again for the support!

reviews.review_description: I have been playing this game on and off for a few weeks and I have been enjoying the game. The puzzle varieties are good and most of the puzzles have been fun to solve. I do wish the graphics were a bit higher resolution and I wish the controls were more dialed in. I have had a few times where I accidentally grabbed the wrong parts of the puzzle. Not the end of the world but annoying. Overall I think this is a 4.5 stars for me . If you like puzzle games, give it a try.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the wonderful 4.5-star review and for recommending the game! I'm so glad to hear you've been enjoying the puzzle variety over the past few weeks.
I'm sorry for the minor frustrations with grabbing the wrong puzzle pieces, and I will definitely look into fine-tuning those grabbing mechanics for future updates. Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts and help improve the game!

reviews.review_description: The free trial worked fine but once I paid to continue the experience changed. Gripping and maneuvering the wheel on the second puzzle does not work despite rebooting, changing controller batteries, or any combination of controls. And there is no one to contact for support and no way I can find to get a fix for the bug or get my money back.

developer_response.body:
Hi! We are so sorry this happened to you. Experiencing a bug right after purchasing the game is the worst! What you are experiencing is an unknown bug, and we want to help you fix it immediately so you can keep playing.

We are here to help! Please contact us directly at our Discord community here:
https://discord.gg/p7tZ5ymKSW
We will give you full support to get past this puzzle or help you out with a refund if we can't get it resolved.

reviews.review_title: Had an absolute blast. Great puzzles.
reviews.review_description:
The difficulty of the puzzles in this game were perfect for me. Often headscratchers but without the feeling of frustration of getting too stuck. The game felt short and sweet with no easy puzzle bloat. Took a few hours to complete.

The downside is that there are a few issues with the controls which can make certain puzzles frustrating (accidentally clicking the wrong piece for instance). The devs have assured me that they're looking into this, and I'll happily update this review once it's fixed :)

Even with the controls issue, I highly recommend this game for any puzzle lover

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words and for taking the time to share your experience! It’s great to hear that the puzzle difficulty hit that 'sweet spot' for you; keeping the no-busy-work design philosophy front and center was a major goal during development.

Regarding the controls, we really appreciate the specific feedback about accidental clicks. We are investigating ways to refine the interaction system to make the experience as smooth as the puzzles themselves.

Thank you for your patience and for recommending the game to others. We’re glad you had a blast in the desert :)

reviews.review_description:
But then ... I was ALL done with the first scenario and was working on the side where you have to turn over multiple tiles - (seemed like there was a faint lion picture on the top of the tiles).  I couldn't figure out what I was supposed to be accomplishing - unlike the other puzzles which were self explanatory or better yet - popped up with clues.

I decided to check out the wrist device AND got sent ALL THE WAY BACK TO THE VERY BEGINNING with absolutely NO WAY of getting back to the point I was at.  I tried quitting the game and restarting it - NOPE. Wants me to do the puzzles all over again.

NOT HAPPY

developer_response.body:
​I am truly sorry you had this experience!
Losing progress is incredibly frustrating, and that is certainly not the experience we want you to have.
​It sounds like you may have accidentally triggered an early checkpoint. If you can see the tattoos on your hands, your progress is actually saved! To get back to where you were, simply open the starting box and retrieve the bracelet again, this should allow you to go back to your furthest point.
​Regarding the lion tile puzzle, If you're still stuck, please join our Discord https://discord.gg/kvH6BjfWr , and we’ll personally help you get sorted out!

reviews.review_description: If you're looking for a puzzle box game, this has more production value, story and care than many others. I played through the first few puzzles and found the hint system solid and the ability to move the puzzles around for ease of comfort helpful. That being said, a few things were finicky, like a key getting stuck for a bit in the puzzle, and not being able to skip a puzzle if you're stuck. For fans of the genre, you'll enjoy the experience.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your kind words!
If you need more assistance to get further in the game, find us on Discord for help.

reviews.review_title: Fun and Challanging. Read full review
reviews.review_description: I gave this game 1 star the first time I played it. It was very glitchy and sucked...I promised I would try the full version when it came out because the maker said they were looking for feedback and what I initially said about the game is what they were looking for in order to fix the specific problems.  They did as promised!  The game is really fun the puzzles are challenging but not so hard you can't figure it out. There is hours of fun inside this game. I would definitely recommend.  Thank you for fixing the glitch parts, it really made a big difference.   I have been playing this game a lot and can't wait until more updates come out.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for giving us a second chance! We remember the early feedback; it is exactly what helps small teams like ours identify specific issues and polish the experience. We are grateful that you took the time to revisit the full version.

We are thrilled to hear that the fixes made such a difference and that the puzzles are hitting that 'challenging but rewarding' sweet spot for you. Knowing that you are getting hours of fun out of the game is the best reward we could ask for!

We are at work on future updates (Tammuz and the goddess have more secrets to reveal), so stay tuned! Thank you for the recommendation and for being such a valuable part of our community.

reviews.review_description: I am not normally one for puzzle games but decided to try this one out, and found it was amazing. Great puzzles with a good story behind it, it made for a great mix of challenge and yet not making it too hard as to seem impossible. My only complaint is that the hints came too soon or were not welcome, and option to switch them off would be nice, as well as a seated mode that changes the player height to be taller so i can play while sitting down, as i sit on my floor when i play seated. Otherwise i would highly recommend this game to anyone who likes puzzles or wants a good challenge. SPOILER ALERT: i would say my favorite puzzle there had to be the sliding block puzzle, or the lock where you make a path for the gem to travel on. the board game section was a 10/10 too.

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you so much for the high praise! Hearing that honestly makes all the hard work worth it!

I really appreciate the specific feedback on the mechanics. We will definitely look into adding a toggle to disable auto-hints so they don't spoil the fun.
So glad you enjoyed the UR board game, it's over 5000 years old and was fun for us to re-imagine with a fresh take.

reviews.review_description: I loved the puzzles, they were worth buying the game. However, the control system was very frustrating in the beginning of the game. Constantly picking up something or turning something near to what I wanted. I played the demo and it was so annoying I nearly didn't buy the game. That being said it was much better on most other puzzles. I did feel that the game was a little short but I still really enjoyed it. Will there be an expansion?

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the wonderful feedback! We are so glad that you gave the full game a chance after the demo, and that the puzzles made it worth it for you.

We completely hear you on the controls early in the game; it's the most experimental part of the game. Trying to select the right object in a small mechanical puzzle can sometimes be a bit finicky, and we really appreciate your patience as you got the hang of it. 
We are currently looking into adding more content. Keep an eye on our Discord or social media for updates!

reviews.review_description: A solid puzzle game. Some of the puzzles require a bit more effort to solve. The story is a pretty interesting addition. The game has two phases - the puzzle phase, which can be completed in less than an hour. And then a “boss fight” that involves a board game, and I’m still not entirely sure what to think about it. The puzzles are really interesting and well-designed - something that actually requires you to use your brain. And then the game turns into a board game that relies primarily on chance... Still, it’s a really interesting game. And I'm hoping we'll see a sequel someday.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing Tammuz! I'm thrilled you enjoyed the story and found the puzzle phase engaging. :) I also really appreciate your honest feedback regarding the UR "boss fight." Blending logic puzzles with chance-based mechanics was an experimental choice; UR is more strategy-oriented than you might think, especially the second stage of the game.
Thank you again for your support!

reviews.review_description:
Really solid puzzle game, the game is just challenging enough to make you feel smart for doing it but not too difficult you'll get stuck and be tearing your hair out in frustration.
I don't want to spoil anything so I'll keep it short, a good variety of puzzle types, probably 3+ hours of game and you get to face off against a boss at the end in a type of board game.
You can also play in VR or MR.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind words!

reviews.review_description:
A challenging puzzle game in an ancient environment. 

Once I started playing, I couldn’t stop. 

You start with a chest. Once you opened it, the challenging puzzles starts.  
It includes also a board game, which was new for me.

And you can play it seated.

Check my gameplay on my YT channel, if you want to see more of the game (or when you need help 😝).

https://youtu.be/4wSFn2YR4HA?si=nJREyG6Np1_xzum-

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind words!

reviews.review_description:
This reminds me a lot of The Room, with the style of puzzles being based around a cube.
Really like how it’s a “sit down” game, and how you can rotate and adjust the height during gameplay at any time… can also switch between MR and VR play.

The puzzles are very good, got me thinking “I can’t do this!” A few times, but I pressed on, and did eventually complete the puzzle.

The game isn’t too long, but is very enjoyable.. was a awkward at times to grab and move parts in various puzzles, especially th e cylinder puzzle, with having to select the part you want to rotate, and then rotate it… was very awkward.

The graphics are nice, puzzle designs great.. has a story too, but I found that dragged on a little at times.. but didn’t detract from the gameplay or puzzles.

Sounds and music was quite nice during the gameplay too.

I’d recommend this to puzzle fans, or those that don’t long games that are that long.

Looking forward to the next game

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the awesome review! 🙏
We’re really glad you enjoyed the puzzles and atmosphere :)
We hear you on the cylinder puzzle controls. We’re already working on making that smoother in an update.
Love that you stuck with it till the end! And don’t worry... the desert isn’t done whispering its secrets just yet... more secrets await beyond the Early Access phase.

reviews.review_title: Great Introduction to VR! Simple, Smart.
reviews.review_description:
I have played a lot of VR games over the years, but this game really impressed me with how accessible it is without feeling shallow. The tactile, hands-on puzzles are easy for beginners to understand, you can touch, twist, and slide things relatively naturally... which makes it perfect for anyone new to VR. 
I let a few friends and their kids to try it as their first VR experience, and they picked it up instantly. No complicated controls, no motion sickness, just clear, satisfying interactions that feel great to solve.

At the same time, as an experienced gamer, I still found the puzzles clever and the lore fascinating. It strikes a rare balance, simple enough for beginners, but rewarding enough for veterans.
I think it could be a perfect “first” VR game that doesn’t talk down to you.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this detailed review! That balance between accessibility and depth was exactly what we aimed for. Really glad to hear it worked both for newcomers and experienced players alike, that means a lot!

reviews.review_description:
An incredibly well made puzzle game with tons and tons of creative and unique puzzles, coupled with a fun story and awesome characters? Sign me up!!
Loved every minute of it, even though I got destroyed in that little game at the end, haha

10/10 - can't wait for the next one!! 👀

developer_response.body: Haha, thank you!! 😄 So glad you enjoyed it! The UR game can be brutal! We put a lot of love into the puzzles and story, so it’s amazing to hear it resonated. We are working hard for the official release and adding more content for you guys :)

reviews.review_title: The final boss was a great character.
reviews.review_description: The game was awesome, and the story too, and I enjoyed it, especially the last puzzle to defeat the final boss, which was great.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind words!

reviews.review_description: There is a lot of variety to the puzzles, they are challenging in just the right amount. I had fun trying to figure out how to progress. I only had issues with grabbing something and it falling over. But overall a solid game I would recommend to anyone who likes solving puzzles

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much! 🙏 Really happy you enjoyed the puzzles. We’re refining the grab mechanics to make interactions feel even smoother in the next update.
We appreciate the feedback and recommendation!

reviews.review_description:
Tammuz is a story about ancient Mesopotamian god and goddess in which we play a character chosen by the last.
The setting is exotic and unusual, which is a change from what we usually see.
Graphically, the game is good. The voice acting is very immersive.
The game can be played by anyone, sitting as standing and can be played in roomscale (no motion sickness issues).
There is also a mixed reality mode.
The game is played entirely with controllers (no hand tracking, maybe later?).

The game is composed of 3 acts and is a puzzle game for the majority of its content with a surprising part for the last one (I won't say more to leave the surprise).
The experience lasts between 3-5 hours depending on your level.
You have a central part from which you will chain the puzzles which are quite different from each other.
The difficulty is perfectly balanced throughout: the game is neither too easy nor too hard. 
Just enough to make people feel smart and not stupid which is quite gratifying.
And it makes you want to chain it all without any sense of weariness.
There is just one puzzle (the first one in the second part with the red marbles) that I find quite hard and quite frustrating and which could put some people off, partly because of the controls.

The controls are also the only weak point.
As much as the first part which corresponds to the demo has practically no problems (corrected with demo feedback), as much as the controls of the 2nd are a little floating/clunky and quite imprecise (or too precise, it all depends on how you see it).
It is worth noting nothing will stop you from progressing but it's regrettable given the undeniable qualities of the game which would have come close to perfection without this.

Note that the game is in EA: the developers are listening and will release a patch to improve the experience, which reflects my rating of 5/5 where indulgence is required.

I really enjoyed the experience, thank you very much!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your thoughtful review and detailed feedback!
We’re thrilled you enjoyed the story, atmosphere, and puzzles, and truly appreciate your kind words about the voice acting and balance.
We’re already working on refining the controls on the Tammuz box based on feedback like yours to make the experience smoother.
Your support means a lot to our small indie team! thank you for exploring the sands with us!

reviews.review_description: A really well made game that makes you feel smarter after solving most of its riddles and puzzles which, for me, is the most important thing in this genre. The game uses a wide range of mechanics and problem solving styles, giving each puzzle its own unique identity. I can confidently recommend it to all puzzle game enthusiasts.

Early impressions so far so good.

Key from the dev but that will not cloud my judgment, 
Played the demo months back and was impressed by what was on offer.

It does look very good too, the tone is set in tbe dessert, to open the box or not to open the box, well of course I did, some intriguing & thought provoking puzzles,  I do love a puzzle game and this is a very good one, great for people that can only sit and play games. Definitely a recommendation if you want to test the old grey matter.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind words!

reviews.review_title: A reluctant 4... in a good way
reviews.review_description: I was lucky enough to win this on win this on Sidequest so I wanted to give feedback without having to give a rating, but the system doesn't let me. I'm aware that this is early access and it would have been 5 stars as it is a beautiful looking puzzler, but I am struggling with controlling it. I've read the reviews left by others, and seen the developers responses and realize that I didn't pay for it... but I can't give 5 stars... simply because I am having so much difficulty with wrong hits on the tabletop puzzle... it feels like I am randomly mashing a complicated piece of equipment as there is no indication as to which part of the hand should be used. I also had trouble with the earlier rotating cylinder but kept at it and managed. A bit more time with the piece we have selected being highlighted and available for adjustment would make a huge difference. The handling issues are my only problem and I believe that when this is fixed... and I trust that it will be... this will be 5 stars... I look forward to the updates and thank Sidequest and the developers.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the thoughtful feedback and for taking the time to share such a detailed review, we really appreciate it!

A new QOL update is no live! 
The good news is that we’ve been listening closely to player feedback, and our latest updates have added several major quality-of-life improvements that directly address some of the issues you mentioned.
To name a few:
- Hand precision upgrades for better hit detection.
- Trigger button interaction that lets you lock items in place while interacting.
- Highlighted puzzle parts so it’s clearer which element is active or adjustable.

We’re thrilled you’re enjoying the visuals and atmosphere, and we’re working hard to make sure the controls feel just as refined. The full release on 1.12 will include even more improvements and polish.

Thank you again for playing and for believing in what we’re building, we can’t wait for you to try the new update!

reviews.review_description: Interesting puzzles that make you think! I've only played a day or two but so far so good, the story line is fun as well and I'm looking forward to finding out more 😊 just when I think I'll never quite get the puzzle - there is the answer, very intuitive! Thanks, well done team!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind words!

reviews.review_description: The game is overall very good: the immersion is successful, the gameplay is fluid, and the universe is well constructed. That said, a few points would deserve to be refined to reach excellence, notably the tablet which remains in the void when you lower the table or conversely which is embedded in it if you raise it. Details remain to be resolved but updates will surely do the rest. A FR update would also be welcome.

developer_response.body:
*Update*
This issue is fixed! It will be live on the next update.
-------
Thank you for your kind words!
As Tammuz is still on early access, we will improve upon the mechanics and handling.

reviews.review_description: This game drops you straight into the heart of an ancient world drenched in mystery, fire, and fate.  Puzzles look amazing and are hard enough to be challanging but not impossible.  Graphics are cool, very crisp.  I did find the handling a little fiddly and not quite accurate at times when moving and sliding levers/controls.  There is a story that adds and builds to the gameplay.  I think this is a must for puzzlers, there is going to be several hours of play here...

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your kind words!
As Tammuz is still on early access, we will improve upon the mechanics and handling.

reviews.review_description: Very cool and challenging puzzler.  Didn’t finish yet but so far I was able to play everything laying down.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for playing and for the kind words!
We’re glad you’re enjoying the puzzles and its awesome to hear it works well even while lying down!
Can’t wait to hear what you think once you reach the end :)

reviews.review_description: wish the hints were a little more clear but I realy enjoyed the game

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing! Glad you enjoyed it 😊 We’re working on improvements to the game based on feedback like yours, we really appreciate it!

reviews.review_description: A wonderfully crafted puzzle experience   yet surprisingly challenging as you progress. Each level feels rewarding, and the difficulty curve is perfectly balanced. A must play for puzzle lovers

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words!

reviews.review_description: I think this game shows that there are still a lot of new experiences to create and discover using VR/MR tech! I am a big puzzle games fan so to me this is just a delight, but also very impressed by how physical everything feels. Good job team!🙏🏼

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind words!
